articles.title: Propensity Score-Matched Comparison of Patient-Reported Outcomes Between Crowe I Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip and Primary Osteoarthritis Following Total Hip Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Saluja A, Eschert J, Ren R, Wong Z, Su E
articles.population: Patients undergoing total hip arthroplasty for either Crowe I developmental dysplasia of the hip or primary osteoarthritis
articles.sample_size: 210 hips (105 with developmental dysplasia of the hip, 105 with osteoarthritis)
articles.outcomes: Modified Harris Hip Score, Hip disability and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score - Joint Replacement, pain scores, activity levels, revision rates, and implant survivorship at minimum 2-year follow-up
articles.take_home: Patients with developmental dysplasia of the hip and those with osteoarthritis experienced similar improvements in pain, function and activity after hip replacement surgery, with comparable revision rates and implant survival. A patient's condition before surgery, rather than which diagnosis they had, was the main factor determining whether they achieved clinically important benefit.

articles.title: What Factors Increase the Risk of Dislocation after Primary Total Hip Arthroplasty Using the Direct Anterior Approach?
articles.authors: Murphy MP, Forti CR, Ho H, Hopper RH Jr, Hamilton WG
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty via direct anterior approach
articles.outcomes: Risk factors for hip dislocation including patient demographics, implant characteristics, and radiographic parameters such as cup orientation and offset
articles.take_home: Women having smaller femoral heads (28 mm or 32 mm) and less consistent cup positioning had higher dislocation rates after hip replacement via the direct anterior approach. Overall dislocation risk with this approach is low at 0.5%.

articles.title: Robotic Total Hip Arthroplasty in Atypical Hip Anatomy: Accuracy of Component Positioning and Clinical Outcomes in 192 Complex Cases.
articles.authors: Kayani B, Enson J, Donaldson J, Miles J, Newman S, Jayadev C, et al.
articles.population: Patients with atypical hip anatomy including developmental dysplasia, Perthes disease, slipped capital femoral epiphysis, previous acetabular fracture, and skeletal dysplasia
articles.outcomes: Component positioning accuracy, patient satisfaction, functional scores (UCLA, WOMAC, Oxford Hip Score, Forgotten Joint Score), component survivorship, and complications
articles.take_home: Robotic hip replacement was highly accurate at positioning components in patients with complex hip anatomy, achieving correct placement in over 94% of cases. Patients reported high satisfaction and good functional improvement, with 98.8% of implants surviving without revision at five-year follow-up.

articles.title: Social Determinants of Health Z-Codes are Associated with Low Compliance with Patient-Reported Outcome Measures after Total Hip Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Parry M, Ozdag Y, Kloc A, Salvato J, Mercuri JJ
articles.population: Patients undergoing elective total hip arthroplasty between 2017 and 2022
articles.outcomes: Compliance with patient-reported outcome measures at two years follow-up; factors associated with completing questionnaires
articles.take_home: Only about a quarter of patients completed outcome questionnaires after hip replacement surgery. Patients with social disadvantage codes, those who do not speak English, and men were less likely to complete questionnaires, whilst married and employed patients were more likely to do so.

articles.title: Differences Between Inpatient and Outpatient Medicare Total Knee Arthroplasty: Substantial Clinical Benefit Thresholds on Knee Injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score for Joint Replacement.
articles.authors: Patel SV, Pasqualini I, Khan ST, Elmenawi KA, Zhang C, Piuzzi NS
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using prospectively collected data
articles.population: Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years and over who had primary elective total knee replacement
articles.outcomes: Knee injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score for Joint Replacement (KOOS-JR) substantial clinical benefit thresholds and achievement rates by care setting
articles.take_home: Patients having knee replacement as inpatients need a bigger improvement in their KOOS-JR score (29.3 points) to count as successful compared to outpatients (21.5 points), likely because inpatients tend to be less healthy at the start. Using different success measures for each setting may be fairer than using one universal threshold for all patients.

articles.title: Risk and Benefit Profile with Concurrent Nonsteroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drugs and Direct Oral Anticoagulants Following Total Knee Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Paul BR, Verhey JT, Haak GM, Braithwaite CL, Tarabichi S, Christopher ZK, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using national claims database
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total knee arthroplasty who received direct oral anticoagulants for blood clot prevention
articles.sample_size: 12,733 patients receiving NSAID plus DOAC and 37,127 receiving DOAC alone
articles.outcomes: 90-day wound complications, surgical site infection, periprosthetic joint infection, bleeding events, 2-year revision rates, arthrofibrosis and manipulation under anaesthesia
articles.take_home: Patients taking NSAIDs together with blood-thinning medication after knee replacement had higher rates of wound problems, infection and need for further surgery, though they had fewer problems with stiffness. The findings suggest careful consideration is needed when prescribing these medications together after knee replacement.

articles.title: Complications of Ceramic-on-Ceramic Total Hip Arthroplasty with Large Heads: 10-Year Minimum Follow-Up.
articles.authors: Lee DH, Park JW, Jung WH, Kim TY, Ha YC, Koo KH, et al.
articles.study_type: Prospective multicenter study with minimum 10-year follow-up
articles.population: Patients undergoing cementless total hip arthroplasty with ceramic-on-ceramic bearings
articles.sample_size: 178 patients (201 hips) followed for mean 12.3 years
articles.outcomes: Ceramic fracture, dislocation, clinical scores, osteolysis, articular noise, and revision surgery rates
articles.take_home: Large ceramic hip implants successfully prevented early dislocations and showed no ceramic fractures over 10 years, with very high survival rates. However, late dislocations linked to spine problems and clicking sounds were still observed, suggesting ongoing monitoring is needed for long-term outcomes.

articles.title: Disparities in Preoperative Weight Loss and Obesity Treatment Before Total Joint Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Seward MW, Grimm JA, Bedard NA, Hannon CP, Berry DJ, Abdel MP
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort analysis of disparities in preoperative obesity management
articles.population: Patients with BMI 30 or above undergoing total hip or knee replacement between 2002 and 2019
articles.sample_size: 6,128 patients with preoperative BMI data (4,953 with socioeconomic status information)
articles.outcomes: Preoperative obesity treatment utilisation (weight loss medications, nutrition services, bariatric surgery), likelihood of weight loss of 5 pounds or more, association between socioeconomic status and BMI and weight loss outcomes
articles.take_home: Very few patients received obesity treatment before joint replacement surgery, and those with lower socioeconomic status and women were less likely to achieve weight loss. Current BMI cutoffs for surgery may unfairly disadvantage women and poorer patients, and better support for weight management before surgery is needed.

articles.title: Construction of a prediction model for isolated distal deep vein thrombosis after total knee arthroplasty based on machine learning.
articles.authors: Chen JH, Zhang ZF, Guo SH
articles.study_type: Prediction model development study using machine learning
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Isolated distal deep vein thrombosis (blood clots in the calf veins) within three days after surgery; model performance assessed by AUC scores
articles.take_home: About 1 in 6 patients develop calf blood clots after knee replacement surgery. Researchers created a prediction tool using seven patient factors to identify which patients are at higher risk, which could help doctors provide preventive treatment to vulnerable patients.

articles.title: Evaluation of the performance of large language models in the management of hip and knee osteoarthritis: analysis of EULAR 2023 recommendations.
articles.authors: Mirza Y, Sari F, Arslan S
articles.population: Large language models (DeepSeek-R1, Gemini 2.5 Flash, ChatGPT-5)
articles.sample_size: 8 recommendations from 2023 EULAR guideline converted into questions
articles.outcomes: Informational quality, reliability, usability, readability, semantic similarity and lexical similarity to EULAR 2023 recommendations for knee and hip osteoarthritis management
articles.take_home: DeepSeek-R1 performed better than other AI models at answering questions about treating knee and hip osteoarthritis, but all three models had readability problems. These AI tools should only be used as helper tools under healthcare professional supervision, not independently by patients.

articles.title: A System in Motion: The Evolution of Orthopaedic Care in Romania.
articles.authors: Dragosloveanu S, Nedelea DG, Scheau C
articles.population: Romanian orthopaedic patients including elderly patients with fragility fractures, oncologic conditions and those undergoing revision surgery
articles.outcomes: Adoption of digital infrastructure and robotic-assisted surgery; hospital bed capacity; modernisation of national arthroplasty register
articles.take_home: Romania's orthopaedic healthcare system is modernising with increased use of digital technology and robotic surgery, and has high hospital capacity to support complex patient care. The country has relaunched its national joint replacement register to match standards in other European countries.

articles.title: Salvage total hip arthroplasty after internal fixation compared with acute total hip arthroplasty for fracture: a cohort study based on 32,960 cases from the Dutch Arthroplasty Register.
articles.authors: Van Marle L, Peters RM, Van Steenbergen LN, Jaarsma RL, Doornberg JN, Zijlstra WP
articles.study_type: Cohort study using national register data
articles.population: Patients who had total hip replacement for hip fracture, either as immediate treatment or after failed internal fixation
articles.sample_size: 32,960 procedures (12,486 salvage replacements after failed fixation; 18,450 acute replacements)
articles.outcomes: Revision surgery rates at 1 and 5 years; reasons for revision surgery
articles.take_home: Patients who had total hip replacement after a failed initial repair had similar revision rates to those who had immediate replacement, suggesting that previous attempted fixation does not make the outcome worse. Both groups had comparable revision rates of around 4.5% at 5 years.

articles.title: A multivariable MRI-based risk model for medial meniscal extrusion progression and treatment failure.
articles.authors: Endara Urresta F, Penaherrera-Carrillo C, Barros Castro A, Zuniga S
articles.study_type: Observational cohort study with internal validation
articles.population: Patients with medial knee pain and medial meniscal displacement on MRI
articles.sample_size: 200 patients followed for a mean of 32.4 months
articles.outcomes: Treatment failure defined as major subsequent surgery, meniscal reintervention, or meaningful deterioration in patient-reported outcomes. Model discrimination and risk stratification into low-, intermediate-, and high-risk groups.
articles.take_home: A new MRI-based risk model that looks at meniscal displacement alongside other knee damage can better predict which patients will need further surgery or have poor results. The model identified low-risk patients with 11.8% failure rates, intermediate-risk with 33.8%, and high-risk with 64% failure rates, helping doctors make better treatment decisions.

articles.title: Comparison of isokinetic strength recovery following ACL reconstruction among graft types.
articles.authors: Schlangen E, Wenzlaff S, Swetkovich R, Arenz A, Miller B, Murphy R, et al.
articles.population: Patients aged 16-35 who underwent primary anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction
articles.sample_size: 262 participants (hamstring tendon 134, quadriceps tendon 67, bone-patellar tendon-bone 27, allograft 34)
articles.outcomes: Isokinetic quadriceps and hamstring muscle strength measured at 3, 6, 9, and 12 months after surgery
articles.take_home: Different graft types used in ACL reconstruction showed different patterns of strength recovery after surgery. Hamstring and allograft grafts recovered quadriceps strength better than quadriceps tendon and bone-patellar tendon-bone grafts, but hamstring grafts showed weaker hamstring strength recovery because the surgery takes muscle from that area.

articles.title: A novel stepwise lateral patellar facetectomy technique in total knee arthroplasty: a comparative clinical and radiological study.
articles.authors: Ozer M, Ardali D, Birinci M, Kemah B, Polat O, Camur S
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 203 patients (99 lateral patellar facetectomy group, 104 non-facetectomy group)
articles.outcomes: Knee Society Score, Oxford score, Kujala score, patellar alignment on X-rays including displacement, lateral displacement, congruence and tilt angles
articles.take_home: Patients who had a lateral patellar facetectomy (removing part of the kneecap's outer edge) during knee replacement surgery had better pain scores and better alignment of the kneecap on X-rays compared to those who had traditional techniques. The facetectomy technique may help reduce problems with kneecap tracking after knee replacement.

articles.title: Replication of the Robotic Evaluation of Articular Laxity (REAL) classification using intraoperative gap data in robot-assisted total knee arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Morita Y, Mylonakis N, Shin J, El-Hassan M, Shichman I, Kenanidis E, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing robot-assisted total knee arthroplasty for osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Distribution of mediolateral imbalance, frequencies of varus REAL classification categories, patient-reported outcomes across REAL categories, and changes in imbalance before and after intraoperative balancing
articles.take_home: Robot-assisted knee replacement surgery significantly improved the balance between the medial and lateral sides of the knee, with final imbalance reduced to about 1 millimetre. The REAL classification system successfully organised intraoperative balance patterns and could help standardise how surgeons assess knee balance across different patient groups.

articles.title: 1-year patient-reported outcomes in patients with a documented GLP-1 agonist prescription history prior to total hip arthroplasty: a retrospective cohort study.
articles.authors: Benyamini B, Khan ST, Elmenawi KA, Patel SV, Deren ME, Piuzzi NS
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty, with and without GLP-1 agonist prescription history
articles.sample_size: 12,749 patients (145 with GLP-1 agonist prescription history)
articles.outcomes: 1-year patient-reported outcomes (HOOS Pain, Physical Function, Joint Replacement scores), patient satisfaction, complication rates, hospital readmission rates
articles.take_home: Patients taking GLP-1 agonists before hip replacement showed no difference in pain relief or satisfaction one year after surgery compared to those not taking these drugs. However, they had fewer hospital readmissions within 90 days but more medical complications during that same period.

articles.title: Comparative Analysis of Immune Cell-Type Abundances in Periprosthetic Tissues Across Arthroplasty Failure Etiologies: Use of Transcriptomic Deconvolution.
articles.authors: Li Y, Wang F, Li Y, Mu W, Ji B, Zhang X, et al.
articles.study_type: Comparative observational study using transcriptomic analysis
articles.population: Patients with failed hip or knee replacements: 135 with periprosthetic joint infection and 50 with aseptic failure
articles.outcomes: Immune cell-type abundance profiles in infected versus aseptic joint failure; infection recurrence rates based on CD8+ T cell to regulatory T cell ratio
articles.take_home: The study found that infected and aseptic joint replacements have different patterns of immune cells present in the tissue around the implant. Patients with infected joints who had higher levels of certain immune cells (CD8+ T cells) compared to regulatory T cells had lower rates of the infection coming back.

articles.title: A Proposed Alternative Sampling Strategy for Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Arthroplasty Patient-Reported Outcome Performance Measure Compliance.
articles.authors: Lyman S, Chin A, Alschuler DM, Brill CO, Fontana M, MacLean C, et al.
articles.population: Hospitals performing total hip and knee replacements, stratified by annual case volume
articles.sample_size: Modelled hospitals with 5, 50, 500 and 5,000 eligible cases annually
articles.outcomes: Minimum sample size needed to achieve statistically valid patient-reported outcome performance measures with 95% confidence interval width of plus or minus 5 percent
articles.take_home: A random sampling approach would require fewer patient responses than the current CMS rule for large hospitals whilst maintaining statistical reliability. This could reduce reporting burden for high-volume hospitals and provide more accurate results compared to the current volume-proportional approach.

articles.title: First Revision Total Hip Arthroplasty for Instability: 10-Year Outcomes and Risk Factors Associated with Re-Revision.
articles.authors: Yapp LZ, Howard LC, Greidanus NV, Garbuz DS, Masri BA, Neufeld ME
articles.population: Patients undergoing first revision total hip arthroplasty for instability
articles.outcomes: 10-year survivorship free from instability-related re-revision (82.2%) and all-cause re-revision (73.6%). Risk factors for re-revision included younger age, higher BMI, abductor deficiency, and constrained liner use.
articles.take_home: About 1 in 5 patients who have revision hip surgery for instability will experience recurrent instability within 10 years, and about 3 in 10 will need further surgery. Dual mobility components and larger femoral heads performed better than constrained liners in preventing recurrent instability.

articles.title: Jumbo Femoral Heads in Total Hip Arthroplasty: Improved Early Stability Without Added Complications.
articles.authors: Kim BI, Cheng R, Grabov E, Debbi EM, Vigdorchik JM, Mayman DJ, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 6,413 hip replacements (5,897 with 36 mm heads, 516 with 40 or 44 mm heads)
articles.outcomes: Hip dislocation risk, periprosthetic joint infection, periprosthetic fracture
articles.take_home: Jumbo femoral heads (40 or 44 mm) reduced the risk of hip dislocation slightly during the first year after surgery compared with standard large heads (36 mm), but this benefit did not last long-term. There was no increase in complications such as infection or fracture with the larger heads.

articles.title: Dogma Under the Knife: Challenging the Status Quo in Primary Total Knee Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Padgett BM, Terhune EB, Chalmers BP, Pagnano MW, Hannon CP
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Patient satisfaction, implant survivorship, and functional outcomes with different surgical approaches including alignment strategies, fixation methods, bearing surfaces, and patellar resurfacing techniques
articles.take_home: Even though knee replacements are very successful overall, about 1 in 5 patients remain unhappy after surgery. The review suggests that tailoring the operation to individual patients rather than following one fixed approach may improve satisfaction whilst keeping implants working well long-term.

articles.title: Revision Total Joint Arthroplasty at the Ambulatory Surgery Center: A Single Institutional Experience.
articles.authors: Acuna AJ, Potluri AS, Yadav AS, Alfonsi S, Forlenza EM, Della Valle CJ
articles.population: Patients undergoing aseptic revision total joint arthroplasty at an ambulatory surgery centre
articles.sample_size: 153 patients (181 enrolled, 28 excluded for inadequate follow-up)
articles.outcomes: Complications within 90 days postoperatively; re-revision rates at final follow-up (mean 3.4 years); survivorship free from re-revision at 2 and 5 years
articles.take_home: Revision joint replacement performed in an outpatient surgery centre had low complication rates, with only 11% of patients experiencing any complication within 90 days and over 94% remaining free from re-revision at five years. With careful patient selection, revision joint replacement may be safely performed in an outpatient setting.

articles.title: The "Distal Femoral Replacement Downgrade": Technique of Using Bicondylar Femoral Cones with a Hinge Total Knee Arthroplasty Revision in Patients Who Have Massive Distal Femoral Bone Loss as an Alternative.
articles.authors: Chalmers BP, Afzal S, Bhatti P, Anatone AJ, Blevins JL, Debbi EM, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing revision knee replacement for severe distal femoral bone loss
articles.outcomes: Survivorship free from revision for aseptic loosening 100% at one and two years; survivorship free from any re-revision 85.1% at one and two years; three knees required reoperation
articles.take_home: A bone-preserving surgical technique using femoral cones with a hinge knee replacement achieved good results in patients with severe femoral bone loss, with no revisions needed for loosening at two years. This approach may be a better alternative to the more extensive distal femoral replacement surgery.

articles.title: Long-term outcomes of a cemented cruciate-retaining total knee arthroplasty: a 10-year follow-up study.
articles.authors: Kalaai S, Schotanus MGM, van Dun B, Most J, Tilman PBJ, Van Haaren EH, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective single-centre cohort study with long-term follow-up
articles.population: Patients who underwent cemented cruciate-retaining total knee arthroplasty (Vanguard system)
articles.outcomes: Implant survival rate, patient-reported outcome measures, radiographic signs, time to revision surgery
articles.take_home: At 13.8 years follow-up, 95.4% of the knee replacements were still in place without needing revision surgery, and patients reported good functional improvements that continued to increase between 5 and 10 years. This suggests the Vanguard knee replacement system is a safe, long-lasting option with few problems.

articles.title: Curved decelerations result in greater frontal-plane knee loading than straight-line tasks.
articles.authors: Galmiche L, Kersting UG, Bill K
articles.population: Healthy recreationally active males
articles.outcomes: Peak external knee abduction moment, ground reaction force, frontal-plane moment arm during different deceleration tasks
articles.take_home: When athletes decelerate while moving in a curved path, their knees experience greater stress than during straight-line deceleration, even at the same speeds. This curved deceleration movement may increase the risk of ACL injury and should be included in training programmes to help protect athletes.

articles.title: Machine learning-based clinical tool for identifying factors associated with symptomatic knee osteoarthritis: the Nagahama study.
articles.authors: Taniguchi M, Nakazato K, Ikezoe T, Tsuboyama T, Ito H, Matsuda S, et al.
articles.population: Adults aged 60 years and over with radiographically confirmed knee osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Development of a clinical assessment tool using 8 modifiable factors to identify symptomatic knee osteoarthritis; model discriminative performance with area under curve of 0.771-0.773
articles.take_home: Researchers created a scoring tool that uses eight modifiable factors including low back pain, depression, muscle strength and quality, and movement range to help identify which patients with knee osteoarthritis are likely to have symptoms. The tool showed moderate ability to distinguish between people with and without symptomatic knee osteoarthritis.

articles.title: Factors associated with substantial preoperative haemaglobin decline in hip fracture patients: a propensity-matched analysis of 1935 cases.
articles.authors: Graif N, Abdel Latif A, Warschawski Y, Elbaz E, Ben-Tov T, Snir N, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study with propensity score matching
articles.population: Hip fracture patients treated at a tertiary centre
articles.outcomes: Preoperative haemoglobin decline, transfusion rates, hospital length of stay
articles.take_home: Nearly half of hip fracture patients experience substantial blood loss before surgery, particularly those with certain fracture types, advanced age over 85, or delayed surgery beyond 24 hours. Patients with substantial preoperative blood loss need more blood transfusions and spend longer in hospital.

articles.title: Early migration following revision total knee arthroplasty with tibial metaphyseal cones: a 2-year prospective RSA cohort study of 25 patients.
articles.authors: Mills K, Belt M, Goosen JHM, Van Hellemondt GG, Heesterbeek PJC
articles.study_type: Prospective cohort study using radiostereometric analysis
articles.population: Patients undergoing revision total knee replacement with tibial metaphyseal cones
articles.outcomes: Tibial component migration measured by total translation and total rotation at 2 years; clinical outcomes including pain, satisfaction and functional scores; adverse events
articles.take_home: Most tibial cone constructs showed low movement at 2 years, but movement varied between patients and some showed higher levels of migration. One patient needed a second revision for loosening.

articles.title: 6-week cryo-compression therapy and opioid use after unicompartmental knee arthroplasty with comparable pain scores: a secondary analysis of a randomized controlled trial.
articles.authors: Biemans H, De Vries AJ, Brouwer RW
articles.population: Adults undergoing unicompartmental knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Pain at rest and during loading, oxycodone consumption, knee function scores, quality of life measures
articles.take_home: Six weeks of cryo-compression therapy after unicompartmental knee replacement did not improve pain or function compared with standard care, though patients using cryo-compression took 46% less oxycodone over the 6-week period with a difference of 3.5 tablets overall.

articles.title: Validation of periprosthetic joint infection after total knee arthroplasty: a cohort study on 423 cases from the Norwegian Arthroplasty Register.
articles.authors: Mossige M, Lutro O, Fenstad AM, Leta TH, Bruun T, Furnes O, et al.
articles.population: Patients who underwent reoperation after primary total knee arthroplasty in Western Norway
articles.sample_size: 423 reoperations (170 reported as periprosthetic joint infection)
articles.outcomes: Sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy of periprosthetic joint infection reporting; validation against Musculoskeletal Infection Society criteria
articles.take_home: Norwegian surgeons accurately report periprosthetic joint infections after knee replacement surgery, with 94% of reported cases confirmed as true infections. This validation supports the reliability of infection data in the Norwegian Arthroplasty Register for research and quality improvement.

articles.title: External validation of machine learning models for estimation of mortality 1, 3, 6, and 12 months after hip fracture on 5,055 consecutive patients.
articles.authors: Mosfeldt M, Jorgensen HL, Lauritzen JB, Jansson KA
articles.study_type: External validation study of machine learning models
articles.population: Patients with hip fractures treated at hospitals in Stockholm, Sweden
articles.outcomes: Mortality at 1, 3, 6, and 12 months after hip fracture; model performance assessed by area under receiver operating characteristic curve, calibration metrics, and decision curve analysis
articles.take_home: Machine learning models, particularly the eXtreme Gradient Boosting model, can reasonably predict who may die within a year of hip fracture and could help doctors discuss risks with patients. The models were adjusted to account for differences between patient populations and are now available online for clinical use.

articles.title: CT-based micromotion analysis for tracking fracture displacement and fragment movement in trochanteric hip fractures: an agreement study.
articles.authors: Aberg H, Hanni M, Sandberg O, Jonsson KB
articles.population: Patients with trochanteric hip fractures treated with cephalomedullary nail
articles.outcomes: Measurement agreement and repeatability of CT-based micromotion analysis for quantifying fracture displacement, surgical reduction, and postoperative fragment movement
articles.take_home: CT-based micromotion analysis can precisely measure hip fracture displacement in three dimensions with excellent repeatability, showing that surgery improved fracture alignment and fragments remained stable during healing.

articles.title: How Do Operating Room Timings Differ Between Anterior and Posterior Approaches for Total Hip Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Ruff G, Di Pauli von Treuheim T, Sarfraz A, Metko K, Rozell JC, Schwarzkopf R, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary unilateral elective total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Operating room timings including patient positioning, device implantation, wound closure, and total operating room time between anterior and posterior approaches
articles.take_home: Anterior approach hip replacement had shorter total operating room time by approximately 10 minutes compared with posterior approach, mainly due to faster set-up, closure and take-down times. However, anterior approach took longer when cement was used to fix the implant.

articles.title: Factor XI and XIa Inhibitors for Venous Thromboembolism Prophylaxis After Total Knee Arthroplasty: A Review From Mechanism to Phase III Trials.
articles.authors: Smitterberg CW, Ando W, Chen AF, Garvin KL, Hamilton WG, Lee GC, et al.
articles.study_type: Review of mechanisms and clinical trials
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Venous thromboembolism prevention, bleeding complications, wound healing and infection risk
articles.take_home: Factor XI and FXIa inhibitors show promise as alternatives to current blood thinners after knee replacement, with early studies suggesting they may reduce blood clots while causing less bleeding than existing treatments. Two large Phase III trials are now underway to confirm whether these newer drugs work better than current options.

articles.title: Intraosseous Sustained-Release Anesthetics Promote Early Ambulation and Functional Recovery after Total Knee Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Huang J, Abdullah Ezzi SH, Wu S, Ali Alshabi MM, Zhang W, Adil MM, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee replacement, mean age 75 years
articles.outcomes: Pain scores at rest and during walking, walking distance, knee bending and range of motion, pain relief medication use
articles.take_home: Adding an injection of sustained-release anaesthetic into the bone of the tibia during knee replacement surgery reduced pain and helped patients walk further and bend their knee better in the first few days after surgery compared to standard local anaesthetic injection alone.

articles.title: Comparing Clinical and Radiological Outcomes for a Three-Dimensional Printed Highly-Porous Cup and a Hydroxyapatite-Coated Non-Highly-Porous Cup in Total Hip Arthroplasty Using Propensity Score Matching.
articles.authors: Okazaki T, Imagama T, Matsuki Y, Kaneoka T, Kawakami T, Sakai T
articles.study_type: Retrospective comparative study with propensity score matching
articles.population: Patients undergoing total hip arthroplasty with CT-based navigation
articles.sample_size: 132 hips (66 in each group after matching)
articles.outcomes: Clinical outcomes using JOA and JHEQ scores; radiological outcomes including cup orientation, migration, loosening, gaps, and radiolucent lines at two years
articles.take_home: Three-dimensional printed highly porous hip cups and traditional hydroxyapatite-coated cups performed similarly at two years, with comparable clinical results and long-term stability. Although the porous cups had more initial gaps between cup and bone, these closed over time and did not affect clinical outcomes.

articles.title: Aspirin is Associated with Improved Venous Thromboembolism Prophylaxis Compared with Anticoagulants After Total Hip Arthroplasty in Patients Who Have Prior Hematologic Malignancy.
articles.authors: Paul BR, Verhey JT, Tarabichi S, Van Schuyver PR, Christopher ZK, Bingham JS, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using national administrative database
articles.population: Patients with prior hematologic malignancy undergoing total hip arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 1,043 patients receiving aspirin matched to 1,484 receiving non-aspirin anticoagulation
articles.outcomes: Primary: 90-day venous thromboembolism (DVT and pulmonary embolism). Secondary: emergency department visits, readmissions, blood transfusions, pneumonia, and mortality
articles.take_home: Aspirin reduced blood clots after hip replacement in cancer patients compared with stronger blood-thinning drugs, and was also associated with fewer hospital readmissions and transfusions. This suggests aspirin may be a safer choice for preventing blood clots in this high-risk group.

articles.title: Adequate canal-filling ratio and extension stem length reduce radiographic loosening in revision total knee arthroplasty using hybrid fixation with grit-blasted, fluted stems.
articles.authors: Shao PW, Lin YC, Chu CT, Chang CH, Hu CC, Lu TW, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing revision total knee replacement for minor bone defects, aseptic reasons, or after infection treatment
articles.sample_size: 79 patients with at least 24 months follow-up
articles.outcomes: Radiographic loosening of the knee prosthesis, defined using the modified Knee Society scoring system
articles.take_home: Using extension stems that fill at least 83% of the femur canal (with length over 20.7 mm) and 77% of the tibia canal (with length over 18.7 mm) reduces the risk of the knee implant becoming loose after revision surgery. Looser implants occurred in 27% of cases, with proper stem sizing being a key factor in preventing this complication.

articles.title: Intraoperative hypotension and short-term outcomes after primary total hip arthroplasty : a retrospective cohort study.
articles.authors: Burbelo A, Clark T, Huffman AB, Scarl N, O'Palko S, Ferguson B, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: 30- and 90-day readmissions, postoperative length of stay, and reoperation within 90 days
articles.take_home: Low blood pressure during hip replacement surgery (measured by a fixed threshold) did not increase the risk of readmission or longer hospital stays, but a relative drop in blood pressure was linked to more reoperations. Blood loss during surgery was a stronger predictor of how long patients stayed in hospital.

articles.title: Outcomes of Total Hip Arthroplasty Based on Surgeon Fellowship and Case Volume.
articles.authors: Katanbaf R, Misch M, Aflatooni JO, Smitterberg CW, Griffin D, Nace J, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective national database analysis using propensity score matching
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Hip dislocation, aseptic loosening, periprosthetic fracture, periprosthetic joint infection, surgical site infection, aseptic revision, and venous thromboembolism up to two years post-surgery
articles.take_home: Patients treated by fellowship-trained surgeons with high surgical volumes had lower rates of hip dislocation and infection-related revisions compared to non-fellowship-trained surgeons. Fellowship training combined with high case volume produces the best outcomes for hip replacement surgery.

articles.title: Functional Outcomes, Complications, and Survivorship After Simultaneous Bilateral Total Hip Arthroplasty: A Retrospective Cohort Study with a Median Follow-up of 4.6 Years.
articles.authors: Diaz Dilernia F, Vasarhelyi E, Lanting B, Naudie D, McCalden R, MacDonald S, et al.
articles.population: Patients with end-stage bilateral hip disease undergoing simultaneous bilateral total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Functional scores, pain, complications, readmissions, implant survivorship at median 4.6 years follow-up
articles.take_home: Patients who had both hips replaced in one operation showed significant improvements in function and pain, with 95.7% of implants surviving without need for revision surgery and a low complication rate of 6.5%.

articles.title: Is a 32 mm glenosphere optimal in reverse shoulder arthroplasty for patients with small glenoid anatomy? : a range of motion simulation study in petite Asian females.
articles.authors: Kiyomatsu H, Fujibuchi T, Kutsuna T, Kinoshita T, Tsuda T, Hayashi M, et al.
articles.population: Asian female patients with small glenoid anatomy undergoing reverse shoulder arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Impingement-free range of motion in extension-flexion, abduction-adduction, and external-internal rotation across different glenosphere diameters (32 mm, 36 mm, 39 mm) and inferior overhang positions (1.5 mm, 3.0 mm, 4.5 mm)
articles.take_home: In patients with small shoulder bone anatomy, using a smaller 32 mm glenosphere with adequate positioning produced the best predicted range of motion compared to larger sizes. The findings suggest this approach may be beneficial for small shoulder joints, but further clinical testing is needed to confirm benefits in real patients.

articles.title: Predictors of meniscal pathology in paediatric chronic anterior cruciate ligament tears: a case-control study.
articles.authors: Urvater M, Johnson N, Bradford K, Carty CP, Astori I
articles.population: Children and adolescents undergoing ACL reconstruction
articles.outcomes: Presence and type of meniscal pathology (lateral meniscus, medial meniscus, or both)
articles.take_home: Meniscal damage occurred in nearly 60% of young people with ACL tears. Young people of Maori and/or Pacific Islander ethnicity and those with higher BMI had a significantly higher risk of meniscal injury, which may need targeted prevention strategies.

articles.title: Posteromedial geometric parameters associated with ramp lesions in ACL-injured knees.
articles.authors: Kemah B, Cacan MA, Koraman E, Korkmaz O, Erdil ME
articles.population: Adults undergoing primary ACL reconstruction with preoperative radiographs and MRI
articles.outcomes: Association between posteromedial knee geometry measurements on MRI and presence of ramp lesions confirmed at arthroscopy
articles.take_home: Certain measurements of the back inner part of the knee on MRI scans were associated with ramp lesions in people with ACL injuries. Two measurements in particular - the posterior medial meniscal posterior offset and medial tibial slope - remained linked to ramp lesions even after accounting for other factors and may help doctors spot these injuries on MRI.

articles.title: A clinical applicable study on lower limb segmentation from CT images for total knee arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Gu R, Bai W, Xie S, Cuomo P, Jones GG, Bull AMJ
articles.study_type: Development and validation of a deep learning segmentation model
articles.population: Healthy individuals, cadavers, and patients with osteoarthritis undergoing robotic total knee arthroplasty planning
articles.sample_size: 205 CT scans for training (17 fully annotated, 188 partially annotated); 40 healthy, 15 cadaveric, and 10 osteoarthritis scans for testing
articles.outcomes: Segmentation accuracy of femur and tibia bones using Dice similarity coefficient, surface distance measurements, and clinical alignment angles (mLDFA and mPTA)
articles.take_home: A machine learning model trained on partially labelled CT scans can accurately segment the thighbone and shinbone for knee replacement surgery planning, with results comparable to manual segmentation and no significant differences in the alignment measurements that surgeons need.

articles.title: Sex-stratified analyses of femoral intercondylar notch morphology and ACL injury risk: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Ho J, Chew E, Chawla A, Patel P, Anand S
articles.population: People with and without anterior cruciate ligament injury, analysed by sex
articles.outcomes: Femoral intercondylar notch width, notch width index, and notch shape in relation to ACL injury
articles.take_home: Narrower notch width in the thighbone was consistently linked to ACL injury in both women and men. The findings suggest that this anatomical feature may be a shared risk factor for ACL injury, though more standardised research is needed to fully understand how it contributes to the higher rates of ACL injury seen in women.

articles.title: Surgical and clinical impact of arithmetic hip-knee-ankle angle and joint line obliquity changes in total knee arthroplasty with hybrid adjusted mechanical alignment.
articles.authors: Song G, Kim YK, Park CH, Kim KI, Song SJ
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty with hybrid adjusted mechanical alignment
articles.outcomes: Changes in hip-knee-ankle angle, joint line obliquity, and coronal plane alignment; requirement for soft tissue release; clinical outcomes including pain and movement
articles.take_home: The hybrid adjusted mechanical alignment approach for knee replacement consistently corrected the angle of the leg and achieved good results in most patients. The amount of change in the hip-knee-ankle angle was the only factor that predicted whether extra soft tissue release was needed during surgery, but alignment changes did not affect patient pain or knee movement after surgery.

articles.title: Does An Isolated Elevated Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate Warrant Further Work-Up for Periprosthetic Joint Infection After Total Joint Arthroplasty?
articles.authors: Antonioli SS, Khury F, Duke AJ, Haider MA, Aggarwal VK, Schwarzkopf R, et al.
articles.population: Patients who had primary total knee or hip replacement for osteoarthritis and were found to have elevated ESR but normal CRP at least 90 days after surgery
articles.sample_size: 641 total knee replacement patients and 252 total hip replacement patients
articles.outcomes: Incidence of periprosthetic joint infection (PJI) diagnosis in patients with isolated elevated ESR and normal CRP
articles.take_home: Patients with raised ESR but normal CRP after joint replacement have a very low risk of infection, around 1 in 100. Further testing for infection is not routinely needed unless the patient also has clinical signs like sudden severe swelling or other symptoms that suggest infection.

articles.title: GNN-based deep surrogate modeling of knee contact mechanics: generalizing neuromuscular control patterns across subjects.
articles.authors: Pan Z, Zuo J, Luo J
articles.study_type: Computational modelling study comparing deep learning approaches
articles.population: Nine subjects providing gait data
articles.outcomes: Correlation with ground truth contact stress predictions (MGN achieved 0.94 correlation versus 0.88 for comparison methods); accuracy of peak stress prediction and identification of high-risk regions in the knee
articles.take_home: A new artificial intelligence method called MeshGraphNet can accurately predict stress inside the knee during walking by learning how individual people move differently, which could help identify injury risks without expensive computer simulations. This approach was better at capturing realistic stress patterns than standard methods, especially at identifying dangerous stress peaks.

articles.title: Nail-to-bicondylar ratio, but not CPAK classification, is associated with arthrosis and deformity progression after retrograde fixator-assisted nailing for distal femoral deformity in metabolic bone disease.
articles.authors: Ozkaya M, Nimetoglu B, Civan M, Balci HI, Eralp L, Sen C, et al.
articles.population: Patients with distal femoral deformities from metabolic bone disease treated with retrograde fixator-assisted nailing
articles.outcomes: Mechanical lateral distal femoral angle progression, osteoarthritis progression (Kellgren-Lawrence grades), joint line obliquity, hip-knee-ankle angle
articles.take_home: A larger nail-to-bicondylar ratio was associated with less progression of deformity and lower risk of osteoarthritis after surgery. The classification system used to describe knee alignment patterns did not predict which patients would progress.

articles.title: Clinical outcomes of hybrid closed-wedge high tibial osteotomy according to the Kellgren-Lawrence grade.
articles.authors: Sonobe T, Yanagisawa S, Hagiwara K, Kimura M, Matsumoto Y
articles.population: Patients undergoing hybrid closed-wedge high tibial osteotomy for knee osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Knee Injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score (KOOS), Tegner Activity Scale, and Lysholm score measured before and after surgery
articles.take_home: All patients showed significant improvement in symptoms and function after the operation, regardless of how severe their osteoarthritis was before surgery. However, patients with more advanced osteoarthritis had smaller improvements compared to those with less severe osteoarthritis.

articles.title: A Concise Update on Decision-Making and Optimal Techniques in Revision Total Hip Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Cohen-Rosenblum A, Heckmann N, Manktelow A, Haddad F
articles.population: Patients undergoing revision total hip replacement
articles.outcomes: Concepts and techniques for revision total hip arthroplasty decision-making and optimal surgical approaches
articles.take_home: This article summarises current best practice guidance from specialist surgeons on how to plan and perform revision hip replacement surgery. It provides an overview of decision-making strategies and surgical techniques to help improve outcomes when patients need their hip replacement revised.

articles.title: Minimal Movement Restrictions Do Not Increase Hip Dislocations Following Total Hip Arthroplasty: A Before-and-After Study of 10,357 Patients.
articles.authors: Klaassen AD, Willigenburg NW, Musters JWQ, Jager J, Poolman RW
articles.population: Patients undergoing total hip arthroplasty between January 2015 and March 2020
articles.sample_size: 10,357 patients (7,666 with strict restrictions, 2,691 with minimal restrictions)
articles.outcomes: Hip dislocation within 90 days after surgery: 1.46% in strict restriction group versus 1.93% in minimal restriction group
articles.take_home: Patients who followed a protocol with minimal movement restrictions after hip replacement surgery did not have more hip dislocations compared to those with strict restrictions. This suggests patients may not need to be as limited in their movements after hip replacement.
